response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. EACH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR². THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS r, THE NEXT IS 1.1r, THEN 1.21r, AND SO ON. THIS FORMS A GEOMETRIC SEQUENCE WHERE EACH TERM IS MULTIPLIED BY 1.1. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF THESE 8 CIRCLES. THE SUM OF THE SERIES IS πr²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + ... + (1.1)¹⁴). THIS GIVES THE TOTAL AREA IN TERMS OF r AND π.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS r*(1.1)^4. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS THEN 2πr*(1.1)^4. TO PLACE TEXT ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE WITH A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER, THE TOTAL LENGTH MUST BE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE INTEGER PART OF (2πr*(1.1)^4) / 0.5. THIS VALUE MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS DETERMINED BY THIS CALCULATION. THE RESULT IS THE HIGHEST INTEGER THAT FITS INTO THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE GAP SIZE. THIS IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE. THE FINAL ANSWER IS THIS INTEGER VALUE. THE TEXT IS EVENLY SPACED WITH A 0.5 CM GAP. THIS ENSURES A VISUALLY BALANCED AND READABLE DESIGN. THE DESIGNER USES TRADITIONAL TYPOGRAPHY TO ADD A CLASSIC TOUCH TO THE MODERN MARKETING ELEMENTS. THIS COMBINATION CREATES A UNIQUE AND ENGAGING PROMOTIONAL POSTER THAT ATTRACTS A WIDE AUDIENCE.
